item.title: What kindness looks like.
item.description: This guy just got hit by a car while riding his bike, and...he didn't freak out at the driver that hit him. Instead, his first reaction was to go over and console the woman that was visibly shaken by the experience. How would you react?!?
item.dc:creator: LowDownDirtyDeceiver

item.title: 9 years off heroin
item.description: Seven years ago I was in prison. I started school for social work to help addicts. Today I am a graduate and in a post graduate school. We do recover.

item.title: Father and sons was randomly searching for an old mining village when he stumble over a deep mining shaft with someone yelling for help from
item.description:
Sweden: The women, who’s been in the hole for an undisclosed amount of time, was pushed by a man when she refused his marriage proposal.
The father who heard her cry for help was shocked to hear a voice from the hole in the middle of the forest. Him and his sons hobby was searching for abandoned places and rumors of an abandoned mining village in the area was the reason why they were walking around in the forest that day.
According to the women’s testimony, she was first raped before she was pushed into the hole, and that the man had returned several times to mock her and throw stones at her while she was stuck in the hole.
The father who found her called emergency services and less than an hour later firefighters was on site to evaluate the situation. In the end a helicopter was used to airlift her out of the mining shaft and she was brought directly to the hospital. The personal believes a thin layer of snow on the bottom of the hole might have dampened her fall of 25 meter (82 feet). The women is unsure for how long she was down there and the hospital reported her being treated for hypothermia.
The man who pushed her has been arrested for both rape as well as attempted murder. The police has also opened up an investigation about his former wife who was said to have disappeared a while ago. Source: https://www.aftonbladet.se/nyheter/a/rERQrw/kvinna-hittad-i-gruvhal-polisen-misstanker-brott
item.dc:creator: BlackDeathZombieSwede

item.title: Don't burn someone who knows where the bodies are buried. UPDATE!
item.description: HOOBOY! Lots of you were right about HR, hell I couldn't even get ahold of them using the normal methods. No matter when you called no one was available or you were outside of the time frame. No one wanted to give me the time of day. So I used the numbers directly on the wall at work and from the good manager. Turns out they aren't up to date either. I called 8 different numbers before I got a real person. AND THEN. He hadn't worked there in a month. However he was more than happy to forward my voicemail to someone else... And then I actually got a call back. He listened, took notes, asked questions. I actually was able to get my boss to shoot herself in the foot by claiming I didn't tell her I was leaving. Thanks the the other manager leaving a voicemail lying about it. So officially according to them, I left without telling them and was told not to come back. However since I did and have the logs stating such, corporate just knows that I was told not to come back after a disagreement. He found that really interesting. So I had to go from employee help, to customer service, to HR guy who hasn't been there in a month, to the Lead District. He was very interested and engaged in what I had to say. Getting a call from higher up on Monday, they're quite interested. Previous post: https://imgur.com/gallery/0ihGSku
item.dc:creator: pixelsushicat
